title: grewia cerocarpa
intro: Grewia cerocarpa is a species of plant in the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the Himalayan box berry or Himalayan box hawthorn. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Himalayan region, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Grewia cerocarpa include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being used in combination with other herbs in traditional remedies.
